questions.text: What is the recommended way to open a live investor pitch?

options.text: "Hello, my name is Ravi and today I will present..."
With a shocking statistic or personal story that highlights the problem
By reading the first slide of the deck word for word
By thanking the panel for their time

questions.explanation: The hook must grab attention immediately. Investors decide in 30 seconds whether to listen. A statistic or story makes them curious.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

The hook must grab attention immediately. Investors decide in 30 seconds whether to listen. A statistic or story makes them curious.

questions.text: According to the 3-Step Answer Framework for Q&A, what should you do immediately after an investor asks a difficult question?

options.text: Start answering immediately to look confident
Pause, acknowledge the question, and rephrase it to buy thinking time
Say "I don't know" and move on
Ask the investor to repeat the question

questions.explanation: Pausing and rephrasing shows you’re listening and gives you 2-3 seconds to formulate a strong answer.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Pausing and rephrasing shows you’re listening and gives you 2-3 seconds to formulate a strong answer.

questions.text: When an investor asks “Why does this need a token?”, which of the following is the strongest answer?

options.text: "Because every Web3 project needs a token."
"The token aligns incentives - freelancers stake it for dispute resolution, and holders earn fees from platform activity, creating a flywheel."
"It will go up in value."
"We haven't thought about it yet."

questions.explanation: A strong answer ties the token to a specific, necessary utility within the protocol, not just speculative value.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

A strong answer ties the token to a specific, necessary utility within the protocol, not just speculative value.

questions.text: What is the most important slide to include when pitching to the MST Grant Committee?

options.text: A "Why MST?" slide that highlights SARAL, WASMify, or low-gas advantages
A slide with your entire Solidity codebase
A slide listing all cryptocurrencies you accept
A slide comparing MST to Bitcoin

questions.explanation: The MST Grant Committee needs to see that your dApp is natively built for MST and leverages its unique features, not just a generic EVM project.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: A

The MST Grant Committee needs to see that your dApp is natively built for MST and leverages its unique features, not just a generic EVM project.

questions.text: If your live demo fails during the pitch, the best immediate reaction is to:

options.text: Apologize profusely and try to fix it for 2 minutes
Acknowledge it briefly, switch to a pre-recorded video or screenshot, and continue without losing momentum
End the pitch early
Blame the internet connection

questions.explanation: Technical glitches happen. Investors judge your composure, not your tech. A prepared fallback (screenshots, video) shows professionalism.
questions.modelAnswer:
✅ Correct Answer: B

Technical glitches happen. Investors judge your composure, not your tech. A prepared fallback (screenshots, video) shows professionalism.

questions.text: "When an investor asks a question you don't know the answer to, the best strategy is to make an educated guess to appear knowledgeable."
"In a live pitch, you should read your slides word for word to ensure you don't miss any important information."

You are pitching your MST-based dApp to a panel. An investor says: "This looks like a solution in search of a problem. Who is actually asking for this?" Write a 4-5 sentence response using the 3-Step Answer Framework (Pause & Acknowledge, Answer Directly, Bridge Back).
questions.explanation: 1 mark: Acknowledges the question without defensiveness.1 mark: Provides a specific, evidence-based answer (e.g., user interviews, testnet data).1 mark: Bridges back to the vision or a key metric.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Guide

1 mark: Acknowledges the question without defensiveness.
1 mark: Provides a specific, evidence-based answer (e.g., user interviews, testnet data).
1 mark: Bridges back to the vision or a key metric.

questions.text: List three specific technical preparations you must make before a live Zoom pitch, and explain why each is critical for an investor presentation.
questions.explanation: 1 mark per preparation (1 mark each for the preparation + explanation). Examples: wired internet, backup demo video, microphone test, etc.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Marks Guide

1 mark per preparation (1 mark each for the preparation + explanation). Examples: wired internet, backup demo video, microphone test, etc.

questions.text: Deliver a 3-minute live pitch of your capstone project (or a chosen MST dApp) via Zoom to the panel, followed by a 5-minute Q&A. You will be graded on the rubric below.
questions.explanation:
Hook & Opening (3 marks): Immediately grabs attention, no filler.
                    Problem & Solution Clarity (4 marks): Problem is visceral, solution is clear and demo-ready.
                    Market & Traction (3 marks): Numbers, sources, and evidence of traction presented convincingly.
                    Tokenomics & Business Model (3 marks): Token utility, allocation, and value accrual explained in simple terms.
                    Why MST? & Competitive Moats (3 marks): Clear, specific advantage using MST features.
                    Ask & Closing (2 marks): Exact amount, allocation, milestones; strong, confident close.
                    Q&A Handling (4 marks): Uses 3-step framework; answers are concise, honest, and bridge back to vision.
                    Delivery & Body Language (2 marks): Eye contact, pacing, gestures, professional setup.
questions.modelAnswer:
📊 Live Pitch Rubric (20 Marks)

Hook & Opening (3 marks): Immediately grabs attention, no filler.
Problem & Solution Clarity (4 marks): Problem is visceral, solution is clear and demo-ready.
Market & Traction (3 marks): Numbers, sources, and evidence of traction presented convincingly.
Tokenomics & Business Model (3 marks): Token utility, allocation, and value accrual explained in simple terms.
Why MST? & Competitive Moats (3 marks): Clear, specific advantage using MST features.
Ask & Closing (2 marks): Exact amount, allocation, milestones; strong, confident close.
Q&A Handling (4 marks): Uses 3-step framework; answers are concise, honest, and bridge back to vision.
Delivery & Body Language (2 marks): Eye contact, pacing, gestures, professional setup.

Note: Your slides must be shared on screen. A failure to share or a technical glitch without a fallback will result in a deduction of up to 3 marks.
